expected_curve: monotone increasing in GATE_BYTES (has_axis crash count flat-high; lacks_axis decays ~1/256^GATE_BYTES)

acceptance.rule: has_axis (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) >= lacks_axis (naive, value_profile) at every GATE_BYTES; parity at GATE_BYTES=1; lacks_axis -> 0 while has_axis stays >0 from GATE_BYTES>=4

design_basis: The cluster's shared mechanism is a single all-or-nothing equality on an opaque contiguous literal; I2S substitutes the whole literal in one mutation while blind/Hamming search must brute-force it at ~1/256^N. Sweeping the literal width N is therefore the program-feature axis that scales the I2S advantage. Spanning {1,2,3,4,5} covers every width observed in members (1-byte switch/enum/color_type, 3-byte BOM, 4-byte FOURCC/scheme, 5-byte 'xmlns'); 8 is the saturation extreme.
notes: GATE_BYTES=1: ~1/256 — all four fuzzers expected to crash quickly (parity).
GATE_BYTES=2: ~1/65k — has_axis instant; naive/value_profile still find within 600s.
GATE_BYTES=3: ~1/16.7M (UTF-8-BOM width) — has_axis instant; lacks_axis at the edge.
GATE_BYTES=4: ~1/4.3e9 (FOURCC/scheme width) — has_axis instant; naive/value_profile expected ~0.

value_profile is in lacks_axis even though it has CMP_MAP feedback: the gate is an opaque equality, so the Hamming gradient is flat (every wrong value equidistant) and value_profile tracks naive, not cmplog — this is the cluster's defining observation (libpng_7294 shows vp 2/10 vs vpc 10/10).
